---
title: "Migrating from SELMA API v2 to v3"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Migrating from SELMA API v2 to v3}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>", eval = FALSE)
```

SELMA v3 is a significant API revision. This vignette documents every breaking
change that affects selmaR users upgrading from a SELMA v2 instance to v3.

## Quick start

```r
# v2 (explicit)
con <- selma_connect(api_version = "v2")

# v3 (explicit)
con <- selma_connect(api_version = "v3")

# Auto-detect (default) — probes the API at connect time
con <- selma_connect()
```

The detected version is stored on the connection object and drives all
subsequent requests. You can check it at any time:

```r
con$api_version  # "v2" or "v3"
print(con)       # shows version in summary
```

---

## Structural changes

| | v2 | v3 |
|---|---|---|
| URL path prefix | `/app/` | `/api/` |
| Pagination member key | `hydra:member` | `member` |
| Pagination total key | `hydra:totalItems` | `totalItems` |
| Auth endpoint | `api/login_check` | `api/auth` |
| Field casing (raw API) | Inconsistent (camelCase / PascalCase) | snake_case |

These are handled automatically by selmaR — you do not need to change any
code to account for them. If your SELMA instance is upgraded from v2 to v3,
re-run `selma_connect()` and selmaR will auto-detect the new version.

---

## Removed endpoint

`selma_intake_enrolments()` is not available on v3. The
`/app/intake_enrolments` endpoint was removed; the equivalent data is now
accessed by filtering the enrolments endpoint directly.

```r
# v2
ie <- selma_intake_enrolments(intake_id = 123)

# v3 equivalent
enrolments <- selma_enrolments(intake_id = "123")
```

---

## Query parameter changes

Several entity functions accept filter parameters whose API names differ
between versions. selmaR maps them automatically — the R function signature
is unchanged. Parameters not supported in v3 emit a `cli_warn()` and are
ignored rather than silently sending a wrong parameter.

### `selma_students()`

| R argument | v2 API param | v3 API param |
|---|---|---|
| `forename` | `forename` | `first_name` |
| `email1` | `email1` | `email_primary` |
| `dob` | `dob` | `date_of_birth` |
| `third_party_id` | `ThirdPartyID1` | `other_id_1` |
| `third_party_id2` | `ThirdPartyID2` | `other_id_2` |
| `organisation` | `Organisation` | *(not supported — warns)* |
| `surname` | `surname` | `surname` *(unchanged)* |

### `selma_enrolments()`

v2 had no server-side filter parameters. v3 adds `intake_id` and
`student_id`:

```r
# v3 only — returns 404 or full dataset on v2
enrolments <- selma_enrolments(intake_id = "123")
enrolments <- selma_enrolments(student_id = "456")
```

### `selma_components()`

| R argument | v2 API param | v3 API param |
|---|---|---|
| `enrol_id` | `enrolid` | `enrolment` |
| `student_id` | `studentid` | *(not supported — warns)* |

### `selma_intakes()`

| R argument | v2 API param | v3 API param |
|---|---|---|
| `start_before` | `intakestartdate[before]` | `start_date[before]` |
| `start_after` | `intakestartdate[after]` | `start_date[after]` |
| `prog_id` | `ProgID` | *(not supported — warns)* |
| `status` | `intakestatus` | *(not supported — warns)* |

### `selma_programmes()`

| R argument | v2 API param | v3 API param |
|---|---|---|
| `status` | `progstatus` | *(not supported — warns)* |

---

## Field name changes

The returned tibble column names differ between versions because the SELMA
v3 API changed field names. selmaR does not translate field names — it
surfaces what the API returns after `clean_names()`.

### Students

Key renames (v2 column → v3 column):

| v2 | v3 |
|---|---|
| `forename` | `first_name` |
| `middlename` | `middle_name` |
| `surname` | `surname` *(unchanged)* |
| `preferredname` | `preferred_name` |
| `previousname` | `previous_name` |
| `dob` | `date_of_birth` |
| `mobilephone` | `phone_mobile` |
| `secondaryphone` | `phone_home` |
| `email1` | `email_primary` |
| `email2` | `email_secondary` |
| `countryofbirth` | `country_of_birth` |
| `third_party_id` | `other_id_1` |
| `third_party_id2` | `other_id_2` |
| `disabilitydetails` | `disability_details` |
| `medicalcondition` | `medical_condition` |
| `bank_acc` | `bank_account` |

Fields present in v2 but **removed in v3**: `nsn`, `contacts`, `initials`,
`identitychecked`, `international`, `mainagent`, `austresidency`, `residency`,
`residentialstatus`, `passportnumber`, `passportexpiry`, `visatype`,
`visanumber`, `visaexpiry`, `ethnicity1`–`3`, `iwi1`–`3`, `lms_isuser`,
`validate_data_for`.

Fields **new in v3** (not in v2): `pronoun`, `contact_id`, `user_name`,
`email_school`, `phone_work`, `homestay`, `primary_learning_style`,
`secondary_learning_style`, `student_identifier`,
`new_zealand_student_extension`, `australia_student_extension`.

### Enrolments

Key renames:

| v2 | v3 |
|---|---|
| `student_id` | `student` *(IRI reference)* |
| `intake_id` | `intake` *(IRI reference)* |
| `enrstartdate` | `start_date` |
| `enrenddate` | `end_date` |
| `enrstatus` | `enrolment_status` |
| `enrstatusdate` | `enrolment_status_date` |
| `fundingsource` | `enrolment_payer_type_id` |
| `enrwithdrawalreason` | `withdrawal_reason` |
| `enrwithdrawaldate` | `withdrawal_date` |
| `enrcompletiondate` | `finished_date` |

### Enrolment components

The component fields were substantially restructured. Key renames:

| v2 | v3 |
|---|---|
| `compenrid` | `id` |
| `enrolid` | `enrolment` *(IRI reference)* |
| `compid` | `component` *(IRI reference)* |
| `compenrstartdate` | `start_date` |
| `compenrenddate` | `end_date` |
| `compenrduedate` | `due_date` |
| `compenrstatus` | `enrolment_status` |
| `compenrwithdrawaldate` | `withdrawal_date` |
| `compenrcompletiondate` | `completion_date` |
| `compenrextensiondate` | `extension_date` |
| `compenrgrade` | *(via `enrolment_component_grades`)* |

Many v2 user-defined fields (`userfieldchar1` etc.) are replaced by
`custom_field_values` in v3.

### Intakes

| v2 | v3 |
|---|---|
| `intakeid` | `id` |
| `intakecode` | `code` |
| `intake_name` | `name` |
| `intakestartdate` | `start_date` |
| `intakeenddate` | `end_date` |
| `intakestatus` | `intake_status` *(nested object)* |
| `progid` | `programme` *(IRI reference)* |

### Programmes

| v2 | v3 |
|---|---|
| `progid` | `id` |
| `progcode` | `code` |
| `progtitle` | `title` |
| `progversion` | `version` |
| `progdescription` | `description` |
| `progefts` | *(removed; see duration fields)* |
| `progstatus` | *(removed; filter no longer available)* |

---

## Updating code that references column names

Because column names differ, any downstream code that references specific
columns by name will need updating when you switch from v2 to v3. The
recommended approach is to write your column references against the version
you are on, and update them when migrating:

```r
# v2
students |> dplyr::select(id, forename, surname, dob, email1)

# v3
students |> dplyr::select(id, first_name, surname, date_of_birth, email_primary)
```

If you need to support both versions in the same codebase, check
`con$api_version`:

```r
name_col <- if (con$api_version == "v3") "first_name" else "forename"
students[[name_col]]
```

---

## Schema validation warnings

When the live API returns fewer fields than the package's built-in schema
snapshot (derived from the OpenAPI specs shipped with the package), selmaR
emits a `cli_warn()` listing the missing fields. This is a signal to check
whether your SELMA instance has been upgraded and whether a package update
is available — not an error.

```
Warning: SELMA students (v2): 3 expected fields missing from response.
ℹ forename, dob, email1
ℹ The SELMA API may have changed. Check for a package update.
```

These warnings can be suppressed with `suppressWarnings()` if you have
confirmed the field removals are expected for your instance.
